notes: Each movie uses a customized relationship schema based on its social setting. The global_categories field can be used later to map movie-specific labels into broader comparable categories.

titanic.schema_focus: Class hierarchy, formal engagement, family obligation, romantic intimacy, service relations, and conflict across social rank.

definitions.familial: A family relationship, such as parent-child or other explicitly stated family tie.
definitions.romantic_intent: Attraction, flirtation, emotional intimacy, or romantic attachment without a formal engagement or marriage expectation.
definitions.formal_engagement: A socially recognized romantic commitment, fiance/fiancee relation, expected marriage bond, or marriage pressure shaped by family or class expectations.
definitions.friend: A familiar and supportive peer relationship without stronger romance, family, authority, service, or conflict cues.
definitions.acquaintance: The characters interact or know each other, but the local dialogue does not clearly show a stronger relationship type.
definitions.ship_authority: A role-based command relation involving officers, crew, guards, or institutional authority aboard the ship.
definitions.class_service: A class-based or servant/service relationship where class status, service role, formal address, or deference shapes the interaction.
definitions.adversarial: A hostile, oppositional, coercive, threatening, or conflict-driven relationship in the local dialogue.
definitions.ally: A cooperative or protective relationship where one character supports, defends, or helps another, often across class boundaries or danger.
definitions.unclear: Not enough evidence in the provided local dialogue context to infer a reliable relationship.

priority_rules: Use formal_engagement over romantic_intent when marriage, fiance/fiancee status, or family/class marriage pressure is explicit.
Use class_service when class status or service role is central, even if the tone is friendly.
Use ship_authority for officer, crew, guard, or emergency command relations.
Use unclear rather than relying on outside knowledge of Titanic.

the godfather.schema_focus: Family hierarchy, patriarchal authority, loyalty, organized-crime rank, obligation, alliance, negotiation, and adversarial power relations.

definitions.blood_family: A direct blood-family relationship or explicit family tie.
definitions.marriage_family: A family relationship through marriage or in-law obligation.
definitions.patriarchal_authority: A family-centered authority relation shaped by seniority, patriarchal command, family duty, or deference to a family head.
definitions.organized_crime_authority: A rank-based or patronage-based relation within organized crime, including boss-subordinate, client-patron, or command relationships.
definitions.loyal_ally: A relationship defined by loyalty, protection, strategic cooperation, or active alignment with another character.
definitions.business_acquaintance: A transactional or negotiation-based relationship where characters know or deal with each other but are not clearly close, loyal, or hostile.
definitions.friend: A familiar personal relationship without stronger family, authority, loyalty, or conflict cues.
definitions.adversarial: A hostile, coercive, threatening, betraying, or openly oppositional relationship.
definitions.unclear: Not enough evidence in the provided local dialogue context to infer a reliable relationship.

priority_rules: Use patriarchal_authority when family hierarchy and deference are central.
Use organized_crime_authority when rank, patronage, command, or criminal organizational power is central.
Use loyal_ally for loyalty and protection, not merely casual friendliness.
Use adversarial for threats, coercion, betrayal, or negotiation under explicit hostility.

dead poets society.schema_focus: Teacher-student authority, mentorship, school discipline, peer friendship, family authority, support, and conflict with institutional expectations.

definitions.teacher_student_authority: A school-based authority relation between teacher, administrator, or school institution and student.
definitions.mentor_student: A guidance-centered relation where one character encourages, teaches, inspires, or supports another beyond simple authority.
definitions.peer_friend: A familiar peer relationship among students or similar-status characters, often casual or supportive.
definitions.familial_authority: A parent-child or family relationship where obligation, control, expectation, or parental authority is central.
definitions.ally: A supportive relation where one character actively helps, protects, or stands with another against pressure.
definitions.adversarial: A confrontational, resistant, accusatory, or conflict-driven relation in the local dialogue.
definitions.acquaintance: A known but weakly specified relation without enough evidence for a stronger label.
definitions.unclear: Not enough evidence in the provided local dialogue context to infer a reliable relationship.

priority_rules: Use mentor_student when guidance, inspiration, or encouragement is central, even if authority is also present.
Use teacher_student_authority when institutional role, discipline, evaluation, or command is central.
Use familial_authority for parent-child control or family expectation.
Use peer_friend for student-to-student friendship when no stronger conflict or alliance cue dominates.

amadeus.schema_focus: Court hierarchy, artistic rivalry, patronage, professional dependence, admiration, jealousy, institutional authority, and strategic cooperation.

definitions.court_authority: A court, emperor, church, noble, or institutional authority relation shaped by rank or command.
definitions.patron_artist: A patronage or professional-dependence relation where artistic opportunity, payment, status, or approval shapes the interaction.
definitions.artistic_rivalry: A competitive relation centered on artistic status, jealousy, reputation, or professional comparison.
definitions.professional_ally: A cooperative professional or artistic relationship where characters work together or support each other's goals.
definitions.friend: A familiar supportive relationship without stronger patronage, rank, rivalry, or conflict cues.
definitions.class_service: A service or rank-based relation where class difference, social status, or service role shapes the interaction.
definitions.acquaintance: A known but weakly specified social or professional relation.
definitions.adversarial: A hostile, undermining, accusatory, jealous, or openly oppositional relationship.
definitions.unclear: Not enough evidence in the provided local dialogue context to infer a reliable relationship.

priority_rules: Use court_authority for rank-based court, church, imperial, or institutional power.
Use patron_artist when opportunity, payment, commission, approval, or professional dependence is central.
Use artistic_rivalry when competition, jealousy, reputation, or professional comparison is central.
Use adversarial when rivalry becomes explicit hostility, accusation, or sabotage.

gladiator.schema_focus: Military command, imperial authority, slavery, loyalty, rebellion, rivalry, revenge, family memory, and survival-based alliance.

definitions.military_authority: A commander-soldier, officer-subordinate, or military command relation.
definitions.imperial_authority: An emperor-subject, ruler-servant, political command, or imperial power relation.
definitions.enslaver_enslaved: A relation shaped by slavery, ownership, captivity, forced labor, or extreme status domination.
definitions.battle_ally: A cooperation, loyalty, protection, or shared-resistance relation formed through combat, survival, or rebellion.
definitions.familial: An explicitly stated family relationship or family-memory relation.
definitions.friend: A familiar supportive relationship without stronger military, imperial, slavery, alliance, or conflict cues.
definitions.adversarial: A hostile, threatening, revenge-driven, rival, or directly oppositional relationship.
definitions.unclear: Not enough evidence in the provided local dialogue context to infer a reliable relationship.

priority_rules: Use military_authority for command within military hierarchy.
Use imperial_authority for ruler-subject or political authority.
Use enslaver_enslaved when captivity, ownership, or forced domination is central.
Use battle_ally for loyalty or cooperation under battle, survival, or resistance pressure.

an officer and a gentleman.schema_focus: Military training hierarchy, instructor-trainee authority, peer support, romance, discipline, conflict, and social pressure.

definitions.instructor_trainee_authority: A training, drill instructor, officer, rank, discipline, command, or evaluation relation.
definitions.military_peer: A peer relationship among trainees, officers, or military-adjacent characters of similar status.
definitions.romantic_intent: Attraction, flirtation, dating, emotional intimacy, or romantic pursuit without formal engagement cues.
definitions.friend: A familiar supportive relation without stronger romance, military peer identity, authority, or conflict cues.
definitions.ally: A supportive relation where one character actively helps, protects, or stands with another under training or social pressure.
definitions.adversarial: A confrontational, humiliating, disciplinary, resistant, or personally hostile relationship.
definitions.acquaintance: A known but weakly specified relation without enough evidence for a stronger label.
definitions.unclear: Not enough evidence in the provided local dialogue context to infer a reliable relationship.

priority_rules: Use instructor_trainee_authority when training, discipline, rank, command, or evaluation is central.
Use military_peer for peer relations shaped by shared military training context.
Use romantic_intent for dating, attraction, flirtation, or romantic intimacy.
Use adversarial for humiliation, disciplinary conflict, confrontation, or personal hostility.

mrs brown.schema_focus: Monarchical authority, royal household service, personal loyalty, confidant roles, intimacy across rank, public duty, and social propriety.

definitions.monarch_subject_authority: A queen-subject, royal household authority, government authority, or command relation shaped by rank and monarchy.
definitions.royal_service: A servant, attendant, household, or class-based service relationship within or around royal life.
definitions.confidant_ally: A trusted adviser, emotional supporter, protector, or loyal companion relation across institutional or class pressure.
definitions.friend: A personally warm or familiar relationship without stronger royal authority, service, confidant, romantic, or conflict cues.
definitions.romantic_intent: Emotional intimacy, romantic implication, or attraction beyond service, loyalty, or friendship when supported by local dialogue.
definitions.adversarial: A hostile, oppositional, socially critical, politically resistant, or conflict-driven relationship.
definitions.acquaintance: A known but weakly specified relation without enough evidence for a stronger label.
definitions.unclear: Not enough evidence in the provided local dialogue context to infer a reliable relationship.

priority_rules: Use monarch_subject_authority when rank, command, royal duty, or public authority is central.
Use royal_service when service, household role, attendant role, or class difference is central.
Use confidant_ally when trust, advice, protection, or emotional support is the strongest cue.
Use romantic_intent only when local dialogue suggests intimacy beyond service, loyalty, or friendship.
